# InfoSec-Framework — Audit Assistant Playbook

> A structured audit framework for smart contract security across 8 blockchain ecosystems. Binding architecture that forces AI agents into structured audit behavior. ~42,000+ lines of methodology, attack vectors, and conversation prompts.

## Core

- [README.md](README.md): A cognitive playbook for experienced smart contract auditors.
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): A structured audit framework for smart contract security across 8 blockchain ecosystems. Pure Markdown knowledge graph — no build step, no web framework.
- [report-writing.md](report-writing.md): **Purpose:** Teach auditors how to write reports that judges validate in 60 seconds — and that survive the escalation process afterward.
- [VULNERABILITY_PATTERNS_INTEGRATION.md](VULNERABILITY_PATTERNS_INTEGRATION.md): **Purpose**: Track the integration of ClaudeSkills vulnerability patterns into each audit framework.

## SolidityEVM (Benchmark Framework)

- [SolidityEVM/CLAUDE.md](SolidityEVM/CLAUDE.md): The **benchmark framework** that all other ecosystem frameworks were modeled after. Covers Solidity smart contracts on EVM-compatible chains (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, Polygon, BSC, etc.).
- [SolidityEVM/README.md](SolidityEVM/README.md): **Version:** 3.2 — Integrated with [evmresearch.io](https://evmresearch.io) knowledge graph (300+ notes, 6 knowledge areas), QuillAudits Claude Skills V1 patterns, OWASP SC Top 10 (2025), **[Pashov 
- [SolidityEVM/CommandInstruction.md](SolidityEVM/CommandInstruction.md): **Review: [VERDICT].** [REASONING]. [DUPLICATE REFS if applicable].
- [SolidityEVM/audit-workflow1.md](SolidityEVM/audit-workflow1.md): **Version:** 3.1 — Enhanced with evmresearch.io knowledge graph (300+ notes), CPIMP, Account Abstraction, Transient Storage, L2 Security, 2024–2025 exploit database, and **Pashov Audit Group 170 A
- [SolidityEVM/audit-workflow2.md](SolidityEVM/audit-workflow2.md): **Version:** 3.0 — Enhanced with Specification Completeness Analysis, Compiler Verification, evmresearch.io knowledge graph integration
- [SolidityEVM/Audit_Assistant_Playbook.md](SolidityEVM/Audit_Assistant_Playbook.md): **AI accelerates filtering and formalization.
- [SolidityEVM/vault_audit_guide.md](SolidityEVM/vault_audit_guide.md): **Technique:** Identify unsafe-downcast vulnerabilities inside vault share/asset accounting in under 30 seconds, even in 50,000-line codebases.

### Pashov Audit Group Integration

- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/CLAUDE.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/CLAUDE.md): Pashov Audit Group's parallelized smart contract security audit system. Spawns 4–5 agents scanning 170 atomic attack vectors with confidence scoring and FP gates.
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/README.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/README.md): **Source:** [Pashov Audit Group Skills](https://github.com/pashov/skills) — MIT Licensed
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/SKILL.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/SKILL.md): **Source:** [Pashov Audit Group Skills](https://github.com/pashov/skills) — MIT Licensed
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/agents/adversarial-reasoning-agent.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/agents/adversarial-reasoning-agent.md): **Source:** Pashov Audit Group Skills — adapted for InfoSec-Framework integration
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/agents/vector-scan-agent.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/agents/vector-scan-agent.md): **Source:** Pashov Audit Group Skills — adapted for InfoSec-Framework integration
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-1.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-1.md): 170 total attack vectors
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-2.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-2.md): 170 total attack vectors
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-3.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-3.md): 170 total attack vectors
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-4.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-4.md): 170 total attack vectors
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/finding-validation.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/finding-validation.md): **Source:** Pashov Audit Group Skills — adapted for InfoSec-Framework integration
- [SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/report-formatting.md](SolidityEVM/pashov-skills/report-formatting.md): **Source:** Pashov Audit Group Skills — adapted for InfoSec-Framework integration

## RustBaseSmartContract

- [RustBaseSmartContract/Audit_Assistant_Playbook_Rust.md](RustBaseSmartContract/Audit_Assistant_Playbook_Rust.md): **AI accelerates filtering and formalization.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/CLAUDE.md](RustBaseSmartContract/CLAUDE.md): Covers **CosmWasm**, **Solana/Anchor**, **Substrate**, and general Rust smart contracts. Version 3.3 includes Safe Solana Builder (Frank Castle) patterns, Awesome-Rust-Checker integration, FINDING-FOR
- [RustBaseSmartContract/CommandInstruction-Rust.md](RustBaseSmartContract/CommandInstruction-Rust.md): **Purpose:** Copy this entire file as the system prompt when starting a new audit chat.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/FINDING-FORMAT.md](RustBaseSmartContract/FINDING-FORMAT.md): Ensure findings created follow this format very strictly:
- [RustBaseSmartContract/MULTI-EXPERT.md](RustBaseSmartContract/MULTI-EXPERT.md): ```markdown
- [RustBaseSmartContract/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/README.md): **A complete, standalone methodology for auditing Rust-based smart contracts**
- [RustBaseSmartContract/Rust-Smartcontract-workflow.md](RustBaseSmartContract/Rust-Smartcontract-workflow.md): **Integration Note:** This file contains the Rust-specific audit methodology.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/TRIAGER.md](RustBaseSmartContract/TRIAGER.md): ```markdown

- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl1-overflow-underflow-in-arithmetic-operations.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl1-overflow-underflow-in-arithmetic-operations.md): Integer overflow and underflow in Rust Anchor programs occur when arithmetic on u64/u128/i64 values wraps or panics, leading to corrupted balances, bypassed caps, or exploitable logic. In release buil
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl2-division-by-zero.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl2-division-by-zero.md): Division by zero in an Anchor instruction causes a panic and transaction failure. When the divisor is derived from user-supplied input or an account field, an attacker can trigger a denial-of-service 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl3-arbitrary-rounding.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl3-arbitrary-rounding.md): Inconsistent or unintended rounding in fixed-point arithmetic causes cumulative loss or gain of funds. In Anchor programs handling token amounts or exchange rates, the choice of floor, ceiling, or rou
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl4-saturating-math-misuse-in-financial-contexts.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl4-saturating-math-misuse-in-financial-contexts.md): Using `.saturating_add()` or `.saturating_sub()` in financial contexts silently caps values at `u64::MAX` or `0` instead of signaling an error. This masks real overflow or underflow conditions, leavin
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl5-round-trip-vault-profit-via-precision-gap.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-1-arithmetic-operations/fv-anc-1-cl5-round-trip-vault-profit-via-precision-gap.md): A deposit followed by an immediate withdrawal can extract value when share-to-asset conversion rounds in the depositor's favor at both steps. When both mint and redeem paths round down at the asset le

An attacker can revive the account 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-10-closing-accounts/fv-anc-10-cl2-operations-on-accounts-marked-as-closed.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-10-closing-accounts/fv-anc-10-cl2-operations-on-accounts-marked-as-closed.md): An account bearing a closed discriminator should be rejected at the start of any instruction that would read or mutate it. Failing to check allows a closed account to be passed into an instruction and
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-10-closing-accounts/fv-anc-10-cl3-unintended-closure-by-close-constraint.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-10-closing-accounts/fv-anc-10-cl3-unintended-closure-by-close-constraint.md): Anchor's `close` constraint closes the account and transfers its lamports at the end of the instruction, regardless of what the instruction body does. - [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl1-missing-slippage-tolerance-allowing-extraction.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl1-missing-slippage-tolerance-allowing-extraction.md): Instructions that perform token swaps, liquidity operations, or price-sensitive conversions without a caller-supplied minimum output amount allow extractors to sandwich the transaction. - [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl2-lamport-balance-invariant-violation-in-cpi.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl2-lamport-balance-invariant-violation-in-cpi.md): Solana programs that perform CPIs can inadvertently violate the lamport conservation invariant: the total lamports across all accounts touched by a transaction must be the same before and after. If a 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl3-dust-account-poisoning-blocking-critical-operations.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl3-dust-account-poisoning-blocking-critical-operations.md): An attacker can send a tiny lamport amount to a program-controlled account, increasing its balance above the rent-exempt minimum for zero bytes but below what a later `close` or realloc operation expe
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl4-unbounded-account-collection-causing-dos.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl4-unbounded-account-collection-causing-dos.md): Instructions that iterate over a variable-length collection stored in an account (a Vec of positions, orders, or members) can exhaust compute units when the collection grows large. An attacker can rep
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl5-time-unit-mismatch-in-deadline-validation.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl5-time-unit-mismatch-in-deadline-validation.md): Solana's `Clock` sysvar provides both `unix_timestamp` (seconds since Unix epoch) and `slot` (current slot number). Mixing these two units in deadline or expiry comparisons produces incorrect results:
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl6-reward-accumulator-updated-after-balance-change.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-11-state-management/fv-anc-11-cl6-reward-accumulator-updated-after-balance-change.md): Staking and reward protocols that track per-share reward accumulation must update the global accumulator index before modifying any user's share balance. - [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/README.md): description: Program-level management vulnerabilities including compute budget, upgrade authority, initialization bugs, and logging.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl1-compute-budget-dos-via-user-injected-limit-instruction.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl1-compute-budget-dos-via-user-injected-limit-instruction.md): Solana transactions can include a `SetComputeUnitLimit` instruction that caps the compute units available for the entire transaction. A malicious user can prepend a `SetComputeUnitLimit` with a very l
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl2-uninitialized-vec-capacity-bug.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl2-uninitialized-vec-capacity-bug.md): Creating a `Vec` with `Vec::with_capacity(n)` allocates space for n elements but leaves the length at 0. Accessing elements of a capacity-allocated but not length-populated Vec via index operators wil
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl3-upgrade-authority-not-transferred-or-locked.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl3-upgrade-authority-not-transferred-or-locked.md): Anchor programs deployed with a live upgrade authority can be modified by the authority holder at any time without on-chain governance or timelock. If the upgrade authority is a single keypair rather 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl4-log-truncation-hiding-critical-security-events.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-13-program-management/fv-anc-13-cl4-log-truncation-hiding-critical-security-events.md): Solana transactions are limited in total compute units per transaction. When a program emits extensive logs near the compute limit, the Solana runtime truncates log output silently. Security-critical 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-2-signer-checks/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-2-signer-checks/README.md): description: Verify signer authenticity for secure execution.

Any public key can be
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-2-signer-checks/fv-anc-2-cl2-no-is_signer-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-2-signer-checks/fv-anc-2-cl2-no-is_signer-check.md): When using raw `AccountInfo` or native Solana program patterns, the `is_signer` field on the account info must be explicitly checked. Omitting this check means an instruction accepts any account as an
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/README.md): description: Verify account state and permissions.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl1-trying-to-modify-an-account-without-checking-if-its-writeable.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl1-trying-to-modify-an-account-without-checking-if-its-writeable.md): Solana requires accounts to be marked writable in the transaction's account list before the runtime permits lamport or data changes. Writing to an account not flagged as writable will either panic or 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl10-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-non-zero-data-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl10-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-non-zero-data-check.md): An uninitialized account on Solana has all-zero data. Accepting such an account from `ctx.remaining_accounts` without checking that it contains non-zero data allows an attacker to pass an empty system
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl11-no-reload-after-account-mutation.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl11-no-reload-after-account-mutation.md): In Anchor, after mutating an account via a `load_mut()` or direct field write, subsequent reads of the same account may use a stale in-memory copy. Without explicitly reloading, the program may act on
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl12-not-validating-a-set-address.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl12-not-validating-a-set-address.md): When an instruction stores a new address into an account field (e.g., updating a treasury, fee recipient, or authority), failing to validate that address allows arbitrary pubkeys to be set. This can r
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl13-init-if-needed-without-reinit-guard.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl13-init-if-needed-without-reinit-guard.md): Anchor's `init_if_needed` skips account creation if the account already exists, but it does not prevent an attacker from pre-creating the PDA with malicious initial state. Without a reinitialization g
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl14-realloc-without-zero-init.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl14-realloc-without-zero-init.md): Anchor's `realloc` constraint expands an account's data allocation. When `realloc::zero_init = false`, newly exposed bytes from the previous allocation are not zeroed and may contain stale data from p
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl2-trying-to-access-account-data-without-ownership-checks.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl2-trying-to-access-account-data-without-ownership-checks.md): On Solana, any account can be passed to a program. Without verifying that an account is owned by the expected program, an attacker can substitute a look-alike account controlled by a different program
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl3-usage-of-uncheckedaccount-without-manual-ownership-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl3-usage-of-uncheckedaccount-without-manual-ownership-check.md): `UncheckedAccount<'info>` explicitly bypasses Anchor's automatic ownership and discriminator verification. Any instruction using this type must manually perform the ownership checks that `Account<'inf
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl4-usage-of-uncheckedaccount-without-manual-signer-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl4-usage-of-uncheckedaccount-without-manual-signer-check.md): When `UncheckedAccount<'info>` is used for an account that is expected to authorize an action, the `is_signer` field must be explicitly checked in the instruction body. Anchor does not perform this ch
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl5-no-is_initialized-check-when-operating-on-an-account.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl5-no-is_initialized-check-when-operating-on-an-account.md): Operating on an uninitialized account allows instructions to read garbage data or overwrite state during initialization with attacker-controlled values. Anchor's `#[account(init)]` guards against doub
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl6-missing-account-constraints.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl6-missing-account-constraints.md): Anchor constraints such as `has_one`, `constraint`, and `address` enforce relational correctness between accounts at the framework level. Omitting these constraints forces the instruction body to perf
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl7-duplicate-mutable-accounts.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl7-duplicate-mutable-accounts.md): When two mutable account fields in an Anchor context can be satisfied by the same public key, an attacker can pass the same account for both. Instructions that assume the accounts are distinct (e.g., 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl8-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-manual-ownership-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl8-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-manual-ownership-check.md): `ctx.remaining_accounts` provides raw `AccountInfo` references with no Anchor-enforced constraints. Every account taken from this slice must be manually validated for ownership before its data is read
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl9-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-manual-discriminator-check.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-3-account-ownership-validations/fv-anc-3-cl9-using-ctx.remaining_accounts-without-manual-discriminator-check.md): Accounts from `ctx.remaining_accounts` bypass Anchor's automatic discriminator verification. Without checking the first 8 bytes against the expected Anchor discriminator, an attacker can pass an accou
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/README.md): description: Safeguard Program Derived Addresses (PDAs).
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl1-using-create_program_address.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl1-using-create_program_address.md): `Pubkey::create_program_address` requires a caller-supplied bump seed and does not find the canonical bump. An attacker can supply a non-canonical bump that produces a valid but unintended PDA, bypass
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl2-pda-seed-concatenation-collision.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl2-pda-seed-concatenation-collision.md): When multiple variable-length byte slices are passed as PDA seeds without length disambiguation, different combinations of inputs can produce the same raw seed bytes and therefore the same PDA address
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl3-pda-sharing-single-global-vault.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl3-pda-sharing-single-global-vault.md): A vault PDA derived from constant seeds (e.g., only `b"vault"`) is shared across all users or positions. Compromising any single user's position, or exploiting any instruction that touches the vault, 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl4-pda-purpose-isolation-failure.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl4-pda-purpose-isolation-failure.md): When different instruction handlers derive PDAs using identical or overlapping seed sets, an attacker can substitute one PDA in place of another. Instructions that accept a PDA account by key alone wi
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl5-pda-signer-without-ownership-verification.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-4-pda-security/fv-anc-4-cl5-pda-signer-without-ownership-verification.md): Using `invoke_signed` with PDA seeds to authorize a CPI without first verifying the PDA account is owned by the calling program allows a forged PDA from a different program to authorize operations it 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/README.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/README.md): description: Secure interactions with external programs.
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl1-lack-of-validation-of-external-program-before-cpi.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl1-lack-of-validation-of-external-program-before-cpi.md): When the program ID for a CPI target is passed as an account in the transaction rather than hardcoded, an attacker can substitute a malicious program. Without validating the program ID before calling,
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl10-cross-program-reentrancy-via-callback.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl10-cross-program-reentrancy-via-callback.md): Solana's CPI depth limit does not prevent reentrancy when program A calls program B which calls back into program A at a different instruction before A's state is finalized. Unlike EVM, there is no au
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl11-cpi-to-upgradeable-program-without-version-pin.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl11-cpi-to-upgradeable-program-without-version-pin.md): Calling an upgradeable program via CPI exposes the caller to logic changes introduced by the program's upgrade authority. If the calling protocol does not pin the expected program binary hash or valid
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl2-cpi-without-signer-seeds.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl2-cpi-without-signer-seeds.md): When a CPI requires a PDA to sign on behalf of the program, `invoke_signed` must be called with the correct signer seeds. - [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl3-not-unsetting-signer-status-before-a-cpi.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl3-not-unsetting-signer-status-before-a-cpi.md): When a user-signed account is passed into a CPI, the callee receives it with `is_signer = true`. A malicious or compromised callee can exploit this elevated status to perform privileged operations on 
- [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl4-passing-unnecessary-accounts-to-cpis.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl4-passing-unnecessary-accounts-to-cpis.md): Including accounts in a CPI that the callee does not need expands the trust surface. - [RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl5-sol-balance-drain-via-cpi.md](RustBaseSmartContract/reference/fv-anc-5-cross-program-invocation-cpi/fv-anc-5-cl5-sol-balance-drain-via-cpi.md): When a program passes an account with a SOL balance to an external program via CPI, the callee can debit lamports from that account if it holds write authority. - [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl2-bounce-message-handling.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl2-bounce-message-handling.md): Contracts that send messages but lack a bounce handler - or whose handler misparses the bounced body - leave state permanently inconsistent when the sent message fails, because there is no rollback pa
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl3-deserialization-and-end-parse.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl3-deserialization-and-end-parse.md): Omitting `end_parse()` after deserializing a message or storage slice silently ignores trailing bytes, masking injected extra data, storage corruption, or format version mismatches.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl4-opcode-and-address-handling.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-1-message-handling/fv-ton-1-cl4-opcode-and-address-handling.md): Unhandled or silently accepted unknown opcodes, incorrect bounceable/non-bounceable address flags, and missing workchain validation each create distinct attack surfaces in message routing.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/README.md): description: Prevent unauthorized access, replay attacks, and insecure contract initialization.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl1-recv-external-accept-ordering.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl1-recv-external-accept-ordering.md): Calling `accept_message()` before validating the external message signature and sequence number lets attackers drain the contract's TON balance by flooding it with invalid external messages - each acc
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl2-replay-protection.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl2-replay-protection.md): External message handlers without sequence number validation, or with seqno incremented after execution rather than before, allow valid signed messages to be replayed indefinitely - re-executing trans
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl3-admin-authorization.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl3-admin-authorization.md): Administrative operations (parameter updates, fund withdrawals, pausing, upgrading) that lack a sender address check against the stored admin/owner address allow any contract or wallet to invoke privi
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl4-initialization-security.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-2-access-control/fv-ton-2-cl4-initialization-security.md): Contracts whose initialization function is callable by anyone, or re-callable after first deployment, can have their entire state overwritten - including the admin address - by an attacker who sends t
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/README.md): description: Prevent integer errors, boolean logic inversion, precision loss, and rounding exploitation in FunC/Tact arithmetic.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl1-integer-as-boolean.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl1-integer-as-boolean.md): FunC represents true as `-1` (all bits set) and false as `0`. Code that stores `1` for true and uses bitwise NOT (`~`) to invert it produces `-2`, which is truthy - silently executing the branch that 
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl2-signed-unsigned-confusion.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl2-signed-unsigned-confusion.md): `load_int()` can return negative values; using it for amounts, balances, or sizes that must be non-negative allows a caller to supply a negative value that bypasses positive-amount checks and causes i
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl3-overflow-and-truncation.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl3-overflow-and-truncation.md): While TVM 257-bit integer arithmetic throws on overflow by default, intermediate calculations involving `store_uint`/`load_uint` with smaller widths silently truncate, and unbounded accumulators can o
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl4-precision-and-rounding.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-3-arithmetic-errors/fv-ton-3-cl4-precision-and-rounding.md): Division performed before multiplication, rounding consistently in the user's favor, and precision loss in reward accumulators are systematic value-extraction vectors in TON DeFi contracts.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/README.md): description: Protect against gas draining, incorrect send modes, and storage fee exhaustion that can freeze TON contracts.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl1-forward-ton-amount-validation.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl1-forward-ton-amount-validation.md): A user-controlled `forward_ton_amount` used directly in `send_raw_message` without validating it against `msg_value` lets attackers specify a large forward amount while sending minimal gas - the contr
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl2-send-mode-flags.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl2-send-mode-flags.md): Incorrect `send_raw_message` mode flags can drain the contract's balance (mode 128 without reserve), cause the contract to pay gas for user operations (mode 1 with user-controlled amounts), mask criti
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl3-storage-fee-exhaustion.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl3-storage-fee-exhaustion.md): TON contracts pay continuous storage fees. Without minimum-balance reserves, operations that reduce the balance to near-zero cause the contract to be frozen - permanently inaccessible until someone se
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl4-gas-draining-and-loops.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-4-gas-and-storage/fv-ton-4-cl4-gas-draining-and-loops.md): Spam of invalid external messages drains contract balance via repeated `accept_message()` calls, and unbounded iteration over dictionaries or user-controlled data structures exhausts the transaction g
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/README.md): description: Identify vulnerabilities arising from TON's asynchronous actor model - async reentrancy, race conditions, stale state, and message ordering assumptions.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl1-async-reentrancy.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl1-async-reentrancy.md): TON's actor model enables reentrancy through message ordering rather than call stacks: a contract sends a message and another message arrives before the callback, reading and modifying state between t
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl2-race-conditions-and-ordering.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl2-race-conditions-and-ordering.md): Message ordering between different source contracts is not guaranteed on TON. Only messages from the same sender to the same receiver maintain order via logical time - cross-contract flows can interle
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl3-stale-state-and-partial-execution.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-5-async-execution/fv-ton-5-cl3-stale-state-and-partial-execution.md): In multi-message operations, each sent message creates a separate committed transaction. If any message in the chain fails (bounces or runs out of gas), prior state changes are already committed and r
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/README.md): description: Prevent vulnerabilities in contract deployment, code upgrades, StateInit construction, and contract lifecycle management.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl1-set-code-and-state-migration.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl1-set-code-and-state-migration.md): `set_code()` takes effect only for subsequent transactions, not the current one. Code upgrades without a matching storage migration leave the new code reading old data in the wrong format - silently c
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl2-contract-deployment-stateinit.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl2-contract-deployment-stateinit.md): Child contracts deployed without StateInit are never created, causing messages to bounce or funds to be lost. Address computation using different code or data than the actual StateInit sends messages 
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl3-balance-logic-and-self-destruct.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-6-contract-lifecycle/fv-ton-6-cl3-balance-logic-and-self-destruct.md): Using the contract's raw TON balance for business decisions is manipulable since anyone can send TON to any contract. Send mode flag +32 accidentally included in non-terminal paths destroys the contra
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/README.md): description: Audit Jetton (TEP-74) and NFT (TEP-62) implementations for supply invariants, wallet validation, encoding correctness, and standard compliance.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl1-jetton-wallet-validation.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl1-jetton-wallet-validation.md): Accepting Jetton deposits without verifying the sender is the legitimate wallet (derived from the minter's StateInit) lets attackers send fake `transfer_notification` messages to credit themselves wit
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl2-supply-invariants-and-burn.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl2-supply-invariants-and-burn.md): `total_supply` must be decremented on every burn. If `burn_notification` is never sent by the wallet, or the minter's handler does not update `total_supply`, the supply is permanently inflated - break
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl3-nft-ownership-and-index.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl3-nft-ownership-and-index.md): NFT item ownership can be overwritten by unauthorized parties if the transfer handler lacks a current-owner check, and the collection index can be manipulated to mint duplicate items or overwrite exis
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl4-token-encoding-and-accounting.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-7-token-standards/fv-ton-7-cl4-token-encoding-and-accounting.md): Mixing `store_coins`/`load_coins` (variable-length) with `store_uint`/`load_uint` (fixed-width) on the same field corrupts the cell layout. Using raw contract balance instead of internally tracked dep
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/README.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/README.md): description: Identify Tact-specific vulnerability patterns including ownership enforcement, fallback misuse, struct encoding, and cross-language interoperability.
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl1-ownership-and-access.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl1-ownership-and-access.md): Tact's `Ownable` trait provides ownership infrastructure, but authorization is opt-in per handler. Any `receive()` handler that performs privileged state changes without calling `self.requireOwner()` 
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl2-encoding-and-interoperability.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl2-encoding-and-interoperability.md): Tact structs and messages serialize to cells using Tact's own encoding conventions. When a Tact contract sends messages to a FunC contract (or vice versa), field widths, layout, and op codes must matc
- [TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl3-map-and-init-patterns.md](TON-FunC/reference/fv-ton-8-tact-language/fv-ton-8-cl3-map-and-init-patterns.md): Tact `map<K,V>` types are unbounded by default and lack built-in size tracking. The `init()` function can be replayed post-deployment if no initialized guard is present, overwriting all contract state

- [TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-2.md](TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-2.md): - State update → send message → await callback pattern without state locking
- [TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-3.md](TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/attack-vectors/attack-vectors-3.md): - `store_uint` or `store_int` with a width smaller than the computed value's range

- [TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/judging.md](TON-FunC/ton-auditor-skills/references/judging.md): Each finding passes a false-positive gate, then gets a confidence score (how certain you are it is real).

- [Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/README.md): Sui Move's type system grants objects abilities: `copy`, `drop`, `store`, and `key`. Incorrect ability assignment is a primary source of critical vulnerabilities. This category also covers object wrap
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl1-copy-ability-duplication.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl1-copy-ability-duplication.md): A value-bearing object (coin, NFT, badge, receipt, staking position) granted the `copy` ability can be duplicated by any holder. The attacker duplicates a token to drain pools, inflate supply, or repe
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl2-drop-ability-debt-destruction.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl2-drop-ability-debt-destruction.md): An obligation object (flash loan receipt, debt record, collateral lock, vesting lock) granted the `drop` ability can be silently discarded. A borrower drops the receipt without repaying; a collateral 
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl3-store-ability-wrapping.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl3-store-ability-wrapping.md): A sensitive object (AdminCap, TreasuryCap, UpgradeCap) granted the `store` ability can be wrapped inside any other object and transferred out of the protocol's visibility. The attacker wraps the capab
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl4-object-wrapping-lock.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-1-object-model/fv-mov-1-cl4-object-wrapping-lock.md): A contract wraps a user-owned object inside its own struct (or dynamic field) and provides no guaranteed path to unwrap it. Once wrapped, the inner object loses its independent identity - it cannot be
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/README.md): Sui Move uses the capability object pattern as its primary access control mechanism. This category covers missing capability checks, incorrect visibility modifiers, phantom type bypass, and sender add
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl1-missing-capability-check.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl1-missing-capability-check.md): A function that performs admin or privileged operations (withdraw, mint, pause, config update) does not require a capability object parameter. Any user can call the function and perform admin operatio
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl2-capability-lifecycle.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl2-capability-lifecycle.md): Capability objects (`AdminCap`, `TreasuryCap`) should be created only once during module initialization (`init`). Creating them in any other function, without requiring an existing capability, allows 
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl3-entry-visibility-bypass.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl3-entry-visibility-bypass.md): A function declared `public(package) entry` is intended to be callable only within the package, but the `entry` modifier allows it to be called directly from any transaction. The `entry` keyword overr
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl4-sender-spoofing.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-2-access-control/fv-mov-2-cl4-sender-spoofing.md): A function accepts a `sender: address` parameter to determine the caller's identity, rather than deriving it from `tx_context::sender(ctx)`. Any caller can spoof any address by passing a victim's addr
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/README.md): Sui Move has unique upgrade semantics: `init` does not run on upgrade, struct fields are forward-compatible only, and `UpgradeCap` is the single point of control over future code. Errors here are typi
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl1-init-upgrade-assumptions.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl1-init-upgrade-assumptions.md): In Sui Move, the `init` function runs only once at first deployment. Package upgrades do NOT re-execute `init`. Code that relies on `init` running again to reset state, create new capabilities, or ini
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl2-version-check-missing.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl2-version-check-missing.md): After a package upgrade, old transactions may still call functions from the pre-upgrade package against existing shared objects. Without a `version` field and a per-function version assertion, old and
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl3-struct-field-evolution.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl3-struct-field-evolution.md): Sui Move enforces forward-compatible struct evolution: fields may only be appended, never reordered or removed. Violating this rule causes existing on-chain objects (created by the old code) to fail d
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl4-upgrade-cap-security.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-3-upgrade-safety/fv-mov-3-cl4-upgrade-cap-security.md): The `UpgradeCap` for a Sui package is the single object controlling all future code upgrades. A compromised or malicious holder can deploy arbitrary new code, immediately draining all protocol funds. 
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/README.md): Sui's shared object model and programmable transaction blocks (PTBs) introduce concurrency, composability, and flash-loan attack surfaces not present in owned-object designs. Hot potato pattern enforc
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl1-shared-object-races.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl1-shared-object-races.md): Concurrent transactions targeting the same shared object can interleave reads and writes without a version or sequence check. The second writer overwrites the first writer's result, causing lost updat
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl2-hot-potato-flash-loan.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl2-hot-potato-flash-loan.md): The hot potato pattern enforces flash loan repayment by making the receipt struct have no abilities - the compiler requires it to be consumed in the same PTB. Two failure modes: (1) receipt struct has
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl3-ptb-price-manipulation.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl3-ptb-price-manipulation.md): Sui PTBs allow up to 1024 operations in one atomic transaction. An attacker can compose: (1) borrow via flash loan, (2) manipulate an on-chain price oracle or pool reserve ratio, (3) call the vulnerab
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl4-clock-and-time.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-4-shared-objects-concurrency/fv-mov-4-cl4-clock-and-time.md): Sui provides reliable on-chain time via `clock::timestamp_ms(&clock)`, which returns time in milliseconds. Common failures: (1) `Clock` not used at all; (2) time constants mixed between milliseconds a
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/README.md): Move's integer-only arithmetic, silent bitwise overflow, and unsafe narrowing casts are responsible for multiple multi-million dollar exploits. This category covers every class of numeric bug includin
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl1-bitwise-and-custom-math.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl1-bitwise-and-custom-math.md): Move checks standard arithmetic overflow and aborts, but bitwise left-shift (`<<`) is NOT checked - it silently overflows. This vector directly caused the Cetus $223M exploit: a `checked_shlw` functio
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl2-division-and-underflow.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl2-division-and-underflow.md): Move has no floating-point types. Early division truncates precision: `(amount / total) * price` can round to zero for small amounts, enabling dust exploits. Division by zero causes an abort, which ca
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl3-cast-truncation.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl3-cast-truncation.md): Unlike standard arithmetic, Move's type casts (`value as u64`, `value as u8`) silently truncate high bits without aborting. A u128 value of `2^64 + 100` cast to u64 becomes `100`, bypassing amount che
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl4-rounding-and-constants.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-5-arithmetic-errors/fv-mov-5-cl4-rounding-and-constants.md): Vault and share protocols must round against the user to prevent slow pool drain: deposits should issue fewer shares (round down), withdrawals should return fewer tokens (round up). First-depositor va
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/README.md): Sui separates `Coin<T>` (object with ID) from `Balance<T>` (internal value without ID). Confusing the two, violating supply invariants, or mishandling fees creates silent accounting errors and permane
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl1-coin-balance-confusion.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl1-coin-balance-confusion.md): Sui separates `Coin<T>` (an object with a UID, transferable) from `Balance<T>` (an internal primitive value without an object ID). Mixing these without consistent accounting creates ghost balances - c
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl2-supply-invariant-violations.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl2-supply-invariant-violations.md): Token/share supply must remain invariant: every mint has a corresponding deposit, every burn releases the corresponding asset. Violations include minting shares without receiving tokens, burning token
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl3-fee-accounting.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl3-fee-accounting.md): Fee bypass occurs when an alternate code path (emergency withdraw, admin withdraw, batch operation) skips the fee calculation. Non-atomic fee deduction allows a PTB to skip the fee step. Missing fee w
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl4-dust-and-cleanup.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-6-token-accounting/fv-mov-6-cl4-dust-and-cleanup.md): Three related cleanup failures: (1) tiny token amounts ("dust") prevent object closure, enabling attacker to permanently lock victim accounts; (2) parent objects deleted without removing dynamic field
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/README.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/README.md): This category captures patterns that emerged from 1141 real findings across 200+ Move audits, including named exploits (Cetus, Baptswap, Bluefin, KriyaDEX, Thala Labs). These are high-signal, high-sev
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl1-generic-type-confusion.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl1-generic-type-confusion.md): Unvalidated generic type parameters are the number-one critical vulnerability across real Move audits. An attacker creates a worthless `Coin<FakeUSDC>` and passes it to any function accepting `Coin<T>
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl2-table-collection-bugs.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl2-table-collection-bugs.md): `table::add` aborts on duplicate keys - missing existence checks cause DoS when a user interacts a second time. Move vectors are limited to ~1000 entries - unbounded vectors DoS when full. Both create
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl3-flash-loan-receipt-binding.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl3-flash-loan-receipt-binding.md): Two confirmed critical flash loan findings from real audits: (1) receipt struct has no `pool_id` field - repay function accepts receipts from Pool A to settle loans from Pool B (Cetus, Dexlyn); (2) fl
- [Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl4-real-world-exploits.md](Move/reference/fv-mov-8-advanced-patterns/fv-mov-8-cl4-real-world-exploits.md): Three real-world High/Critical patterns: (1) multi-return functions with same-type values returned in wrong order - silently corrupts all callers (KriyaDEX `get_reserves`); (2) `assert!(x == x)` tauto
- [Move/reference/protocols/amm-dex.md](Move/reference/protocols/amm-dex.md): Applies to: Cetus-style CLMM protocols, KriyaDEX, Turbos, Aftermath AMM, DeepBook, any Move module implementing constant-product or curve invariant swap pools, liquidity managers, position managers, f
- [Move/reference/protocols/governance.md](Move/reference/protocols/governance.md): Applies to: Move-based governance modules, AdminCap and UpgradeCap management, DAO voting on Sui, proposal-and-vote modules, protocol parameter updates, multisig-controlled protocols, any Move module

## Nemesis

- [Nemesis/CLAUDE.md](Nemesis/CLAUDE.md): NEMESIS is a language-agnostic iterative deep-reasoning audit agent. It runs two sub-auditors (Feynman Auditor + State Inconsistency Auditor) in an alternating feedback loop until convergence.
- [Nemesis/NEMESIS_INTEGRATION.md](Nemesis/NEMESIS_INTEGRATION.md): **Purpose:** Track how NEMESIS iterative reasoning is wired into each ecosystem framework.

## ClaudeSkills Plugins

- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/ask-questions-if-underspecified/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/ask-questions-if-underspecified/README.md): Ask the minimum set of clarifying questions needed to avoid wrong work.  
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/audit-context-building/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/audit-context-building/README.md): Build deep architectural context through ultra-granular code analysis before vulnerability hunting.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/building-secure-contracts/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/building-secure-contracts/README.md): Comprehensive smart contract security toolkit based on Trail of Bits' [Building Secure Contracts](https://github.com/crytic/building-secure-contracts) framework.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/burpsuite-project-parser/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/burpsuite-project-parser/README.md): Search and extract data from Burp Suite project files (.burp) for use in Claude
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/claude-in-chrome-troubleshooting/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/claude-in-chrome-troubleshooting/README.md): Diagnose and fix Claude in Chrome MCP extension connectivity issues.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/constant-time-analysis/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/constant-time-analysis/README.md): A portable tool for detecting timing side-channel vulnerabilities in compiled cryptographic code. Analyzes assembly output from multiple compilers and architectures to detect instructions that could l
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/culture-index/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/culture-index/README.md): 130% = STRESS (burnout risk)
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/differential-review/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/differential-review/README.md): Security-focused differential review of code changes with git history analysis and blast radius estimation.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/docs-for-humans-and-ai/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/docs-for-humans-and-ai/README.md): Write and maintain documentation that serves both human readers and AI agents equally well.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/dwarf-expert/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/dwarf-expert/README.md): Interact with and analyze DWARF debug files, understand the DWARF debug format/standard, and write code that parses DWARF data.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/entry-point-analyzer/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/entry-point-analyzer/README.md): A Claude skill for systematically identifying **state-changing** entry points in smart contract codebases to guide security audits.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/firebase-apk-scanner/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/firebase-apk-scanner/README.md): Scan Android APKs for Firebase security misconfigurations including open databases, exposed storage buckets, and authentication bypasses.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/fix-review/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/fix-review/README.md): Verify that code changes address security audit findings without introducing bugs.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/modern-python/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/modern-python/README.md): Modern Python tooling and best practices using uv, ruff, ty, and pytest. Based on patterns from [trailofbits/cookiecutter-python](https://github.com/trailofbits/cookiecutter-python).
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/property-based-testing/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/property-based-testing/README.md): Property-based testing guidance for multiple languages and smart contracts.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/semgrep-rule-creator/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/semgrep-rule-creator/README.md): Create production-quality Semgrep rules for detecting bug patterns and security vulnerabilities.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/semgrep-rule-variant-creator/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/semgrep-rule-variant-creator/README.md): A Claude Code skill for porting existing Semgrep rules to new target languages with proper applicability analysis and test-driven validation.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/sharp-edges/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/sharp-edges/README.md): Identifies error-prone APIs, dangerous configurations, and footgun designs that enable security mistakes through developer confusion, laziness, or malice.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/solidity-auditor/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/solidity-auditor/README.md): A parallelized Solidity security audit across 170 Pashov attack vectors.  
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/spec-to-code-compliance/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/spec-to-code-compliance/README.md): Specification-to-code compliance checker for blockchain audits with evidence-based alignment analysis.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/static-analysis/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/static-analysis/README.md): A comprehensive static analysis toolkit with CodeQL, Semgrep, and SARIF parsing for security vulnerability detection.
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/testing-handbook-skills/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/testing-handbook-skills/README.md): Meta-skill that generates Claude Code skills from the [Trail of Bits Application Security Testing Handbook](https://appsec.guide).
- [ClaudeSkills/plugins/variant-analysis/README.md](ClaudeSkills/plugins/variant-analysis/README.md): Find similar vulnerabilities and bugs across codebases using pattern-based analysis.
